reviews_data.review_text: Talent +Quality, there's a chef in the house. What more can I say?

While the food and drinks here are delightful, the element that truly sets this place apart is the people, those behind the counter, those in the kitchen, and even those dining in. There's something about having a conversation with Santi regarding timepieces, or seeing Jeff's latest pictures from an Argentinian fishing expedition, or watching Chef animatedly discussing politics in French among his friends that just makes the croissant and coffee taste better. Food in its truest form is meant to nourish and supply us for whatever the day holds and the people here do the same.

reviews_data.review_text: Great place and delicious pastries!
Really good

I love going to Renaissance Patisserie! The pastries are so good and always fresh. The workers are really nice too, especially Lindsay and Laurine, they’re always super kind and make it a good experience. Definitely one of my favorite spots to go! Being greeted with a “Bonjour” every time I come in makes my day.

Went in to take a look at their selections, and was honestly disappointed by what I saw. The plain croissants looked like they were drop & bake from a purveyor, not fresh. For $4?? Ripoff. The chocolate croissant looks like the same thing. How can you call yourself a patisserie and not make croissants in house? The food in the display also felt like it had been sitting there for a few days. I will not be back.

I want to start I’m chef of 10 years and I try my best not to hate on local restaurants. But when i get a fruit tray and it’s a PUBLIX fruit tray…. OR i go to a bakery for the a croissant cause it’s the “best in clt” but it’s a frozen sysco croissant is very disappointing. I bought a couple items from there a few times to give benefit of the doubt. But there goodie bags is def “on the last days” and just tryna get rid of product.
My beloved son purchased a delicious cake for my birthday! The taste amazing and the decor was great! Best cake ever yummy! Gourmet Cakes!! Thank you so much ❤️❤️❤️🇺🇸

This place is amazing!! AUTHENTIC French pastries and other baked goods. The chef if actually from France and works in the kitchen to prepare his amazingly delectable goodies. The crab quiche is to die for!! Lots of real crab meat.  Best bakery around. A++

Stopped by Renaissance Patisserie for a slice of cheesecake and coffee. The outdoor seating area with eye-catching yellow cafe umbrellas is the perfect place to enjoy your dessert and refreshment amid your shopping here at the plaza.

If you’re looking for a real French café experience in Charlotte, Renaissance Pâtisserie in SouthPark is it.

I lived in Paris as an American, and my girlfriend is from Lyon — so we know what authentic feels like. Chef Sylvain delivers the genuine article. The espresso is strong and smooth with a proper crema. The croque monsieur is perfectly toasted with rich, balanced béchamel. The almond croissants are buttery, flaky, and not overly sweet — exactly how they should be. Everything tastes fresh, like it came straight from a café in France that morning.

Even the details matter — marble tables, classic café chairs, simple presentation. Nothing feels forced or commercial. It’s relaxed, polished, and unmistakably French.

This isn’t “French-inspired.” It’s the real thing. Five stars, easy.

À French gem 💎 in Charlotte
Friendly efficient service
Authentic fresh food
Tables indoor and outdoor
Very reasonably priced
Great location
Merci Chef

reviews_data.review_text: Was in the Southpark area for an appointment, and was hungry, so I decided to stop by for lunch. I had visited before, maybe a couple years ago, and was underwhelmed then, but I decided to give it another chance. As soon as I came in to order, the girl behind the counter looked “thrilled” to be helping me. I ordered an egg and cheese croissant, which was grossly overpriced at $16. It was rather small, and came with, *maybe* 10 little leaves of salad on the plate. Was that supposed to be garnish, or a salad? The sandwich was ok, a tad on the greasy side, but I was still hungry, so I decided to get a cappuccino and a mini bouche de Noel. The cappuccino was fine-you can’t really mess that up, except for the ridiculous $12 price. The $8 dessert left MUCH to be desired. It looked better than it tasted. The “chocolate” pieces on top were pretty much flavorless, as was the “mousse” on the inside. There was hardly any cake on the inside, and it was like eating mouthfuls of brown cream that was just sweet. It tasted absolutely NOTHING like chocolate, at all. The entire thing was flavorless, and a waste of money. I took maybe 3 bites, and threw the rest of it away. Anyway, this place is overrated, loud, counter girl unfriendly, food is overpriced for what it is, and NOT an authentic, European Pattiserie. I’ve lived in Europe, and spent A LOT of my life there, to know what I’m talking about. They are *majorly* cutting corners in ingredients and freshness, and my guess is, the majority of South-Charlotte folks don’t know what quality is, so they’ll just keep paying those high prices, but also love the *idea* that they’re eating somewhere that’s “French”, so it’s just a status thing. Won’t be back, and don’t recommend.

Still one of the best coffee and pastry in town.  Both indoors and outdoor seating.  Prompt and friendly service. You cannot go wrong with the French baguette or croissant. A short walk from nearby hotels or mall.  It is highly recommended!

reviews_data.owner_answer:
Hello M. Kurnaz,

Thank you for taking the time to share your experience. We regret to hear that your visit didn't meet your expectations. I will happily address your points:

1. We use high-quality beans from world renowned Illy Coffee. I would love to know more about what you didn't enjoy.
2. I am sorry to hear you found our products too sweet. We take extra care to make sure to have a low-level sweetness profile in each of our products to create an authentically French expierence. Please let me know what products you purchased, and I will further investigate what happened.
3. Our croissants are baked fresh daily. I believe the croissants you may have seen were the ones in our display case that we only change twice a week. Please note that this does not reflect the taste, texture, and feel of our award-winning croissants. 

Please feel free to send me an email regarding this issue so I may better assist you.

Sincerley,
Jessica Rivet
C.M.O

reviews_data.review_text: The most amazing pastries in Charlotte and their take away frozen entrees are excellent as well, especially the beef bourguignon. When you pay that much for pastries they should look as good as they taste.

I ordered 4 almond croissants and 4 almond and chocolate croissants and I do not know why the chef changed the recipe for  these croissants. I used to go to this bakery in 2016 when it was located on south blvd and the almond croissants that he made back then were to die for. They were crispy soft and the almond paste inside was something out of this world and I could smell real butter off the croissants. Please chef please go back to that recipe. The croissants I picked up yesterday were dry and slightly burnt. Please chef please, go back to that old recipe for almond croissants and charge $10 each. I would sell my left kidney for those.

reviews_data.review_text:
Bon Jour viewers,

on my day 2 visiting Charlotte, I discover a French bakery next to my AC hotel and I decide to give it a try.  unlike many store bought bakery place, when I walk into the Renaissance patisserie store I witnessed the central kitchen, I know I am walking into something délicieuse.

as any French style bakery, the morning pasty is normally sweet instead of savory, and I order my favorite almond croissant and cappuccino to start my day.  the almond croissant is full moist with almond butter that blend with slightly dryer croissant perfectly.  of course, some will argue it's too moist and sweet, yet I love how it's taste like brownie style.   I was hoping to indulge myself with another pastry but afraid I might fall a sleep if I keeps try all of them.  the Cappucino is a bit standard with a very heavy form build on the top.  I guess it's just alright ~ 👍

the only drawback about this place is the space is a bit tiny for a cafe, maybe comfortably sit between  6~8 and more patio area on the outside.  if you are coming here for few hours to get your job done, you are either coming early or maybe order them to-go and sit somewhere you enjoy.  😉

🇫🇷

Paul

reviews_data.review_text:
One of my favorite places in the US.

I travel all over the country for work and I have learned to have my places here or there. This place is my little secret in Charlotte, by far the best pâtisserie I have found in the US. Second best espresso I have found traveling (and I am Italian, so I have espresso everywhere - the best one is an Italian bar in Connecticut).
Best croissant, best macarons. I seat out here in the sun, have a light and delicious meal, exchange a word with the friendly, smiling, relaxed but attentive, happy team and it reminds me of when I used to live in France.

Chapeau !

reviews_data.review_text: I wanted to try this place, so I went for lunch, I waited like almost 15 minutes for a grilled cheese and tomato soup, that cost me almost $16. reviews_data.review_text: This patisserie has the best quality.  Everything I’ve tried here has been delicious and authentic. This is one of my favorite places in Charlotte, although you can also order to go and take your lovely faire on a picnic as well.
The best coffee, macron, pastries in Charlotte.
Authentic!
I had the blueberry scone, and others had the almond croissant. Everyone thought everything was excellent!
Great selection and always fresh.

The Renaissance did a custom made 60th Birthday Cake for my birthday party, it was fabulous.  It looked exactly like I wanted it, decorated beautiful, and to “top it off” it tasted amazing, it was the “hit of the party.”

Great brunch here! Baked goods to die for. French onion soup perfectly balanced. Fresh orange juice and delicious coffees. Beef bourginon for breakfast is a great idea! Sat outside and talked to other diners. Had a great time. Seating inside is small tables close together but the food is so good it doesn't matter.
Delightful ambiance with comfortable shaded outdoor seating. And the pastries are top-notch! Also, good omelettes for breakfast.

Renaissance Patisserie is guaranteed to take you to the heart of France with their elegant aromas, sounds, and atmosphere. Their award-winning pastries are guaranteed to capture your heart as well.

Make sure you try their giant lychee macaron! It’s literally the size of my hand, and the depth of flavors was a pleasant surprise! The macaron was baked beautifully with a crisp outer layer and a soft, warm inner layer. The filling itself had incredible depth, and the lychee flavor was extremely profound. They did not skimp out on the lychee flavor at all, which was pleasantly surprising. To top it off (quite literally), they added a gold-dusted raspberry on top! At a price point of about $7.50, I think this pastry was definitely worth it.

They also serve a wide variety of sandwiches, soups, stews, and even provide family-portion takeaway meals, in addition to their extensive coffee selection.

FOLLOW @zackaryeats on Instagram and Tiktok to discover more places in NC!

They made my custom birthday cake and it was amazing! It was dairy and gluten free and delicious everyone went crazy over it! Still can’t believe how delicious it was and not overly sweet. Thanks for the best cake ever.

My wife and I have visited Charlotte a few times over the past several years and ever since discovering the Renaissance Patisserie it has become our 1st (and last) destination on our trip.  It is a perfect place for a lite meal after hours of driving.  Everything that we have sampled off of the menu has been great!  On our way home we like to stop and fill up on pastries and macarons to bring back to family.  It is always a special treat and we look forward to it every trip.

reviews_data.review_text: Talent +Quality, there's a chef in the house. What more can I say?

While the food and drinks here are delightful, the element that truly sets this place apart is the people, those behind the counter, those in the kitchen, and even those dining in. There's something about having a conversation with Santi regarding timepieces, or seeing Jeff's latest pictures from an Argentinian fishing expedition, or watching Chef animatedly discussing politics in French among his friends that just makes the croissant and coffee taste better. Food in its truest form is meant to nourish and supply us for whatever the day holds and the people here do the same.

Really good

I love going to Renaissance Patisserie! The pastries are so good and always fresh. The workers are really nice too, especially Lindsay and Laurine, they’re always super kind and make it a good experience. Definitely one of my favorite spots to go! Being greeted with a “Bonjour” every time I come in makes my day.

Went in to take a look at their selections, and was honestly disappointed by what I saw. The plain croissants looked like they were drop & bake from a purveyor, not fresh. For $4?? Ripoff. The chocolate croissant looks like the same thing. How can you call yourself a patisserie and not make croissants in house? The food in the display also felt like it had been sitting there for a few days. I will not be back.

I want to start I’m chef of 10 years and I try my best not to hate on local restaurants. But when i get a fruit tray and it’s a PUBLIX fruit tray…. OR i go to a bakery for the a croissant cause it’s the “best in clt” but it’s a frozen sysco croissant is very disappointing. I bought a couple items from there a few times to give benefit of the doubt. But there goodie bags is def “on the last days” and just tryna get rid of product.
My beloved son purchased a delicious cake for my birthday! The taste amazing and the decor was great! Best cake ever yummy! Gourmet Cakes!! Thank you so much ❤️❤️❤️🇺🇸

This place is amazing!! AUTHENTIC French pastries and other baked goods. The chef if actually from France and works in the kitchen to prepare his amazingly delectable goodies. The crab quiche is to die for!! Lots of real crab meat.  Best bakery around. A++

Stopped by Renaissance Patisserie for a slice of cheesecake and coffee. The outdoor seating area with eye-catching yellow cafe umbrellas is the perfect place to enjoy your dessert and refreshment amid your shopping here at the plaza.

If you’re looking for a real French café experience in Charlotte, Renaissance Pâtisserie in SouthPark is it.

I lived in Paris as an American, and my girlfriend is from Lyon — so we know what authentic feels like. Chef Sylvain delivers the genuine article. The espresso is strong and smooth with a proper crema. The croque monsieur is perfectly toasted with rich, balanced béchamel. The almond croissants are buttery, flaky, and not overly sweet — exactly how they should be. Everything tastes fresh, like it came straight from a café in France that morning.

Even the details matter — marble tables, classic café chairs, simple presentation. Nothing feels forced or commercial. It’s relaxed, polished, and unmistakably French.

This isn’t “French-inspired.” It’s the real thing. Five stars, easy.

À French gem 💎 in Charlotte
Friendly efficient service
Authentic fresh food
Tables indoor and outdoor
Very reasonably priced
Great location
Merci Chef

reviews_data.review_text: Was in the Southpark area for an appointment, and was hungry, so I decided to stop by for lunch. I had visited before, maybe a couple years ago, and was underwhelmed then, but I decided to give it another chance. As soon as I came in to order, the girl behind the counter looked “thrilled” to be helping me. I ordered an egg and cheese croissant, which was grossly overpriced at $16. It was rather small, and came with, *maybe* 10 little leaves of salad on the plate. Was that supposed to be garnish, or a salad? The sandwich was ok, a tad on the greasy side, but I was still hungry, so I decided to get a cappuccino and a mini bouche de Noel. The cappuccino was fine-you can’t really mess that up, except for the ridiculous $12 price. The $8 dessert left MUCH to be desired. It looked better than it tasted. The “chocolate” pieces on top were pretty much flavorless, as was the “mousse” on the inside. There was hardly any cake on the inside, and it was like eating mouthfuls of brown cream that was just sweet. It tasted absolutely NOTHING like chocolate, at all. The entire thing was flavorless, and a waste of money. I took maybe 3 bites, and threw the rest of it away. Anyway, this place is overrated, loud, counter girl unfriendly, food is overpriced for what it is, and NOT an authentic, European Pattiserie. I’ve lived in Europe, and spent A LOT of my life there, to know what I’m talking about. They are *majorly* cutting corners in ingredients and freshness, and my guess is, the majority of South-Charlotte folks don’t know what quality is, so they’ll just keep paying those high prices, but also love the *idea* that they’re eating somewhere that’s “French”, so it’s just a status thing. Won’t be back, and don’t recommend.

Still one of the best coffee and pastry in town.  Both indoors and outdoor seating.  Prompt and friendly service. You cannot go wrong with the French baguette or croissant. A short walk from nearby hotels or mall.  It is highly recommended!

the only drawback about this place is the space is a bit tiny for a cafe, maybe comfortably sit between  6~8 and more patio area on the outside.  if you are coming here for few hours to get your job done, you are either coming early or maybe order them to-go and sit somewhere you enjoy.  😉

🇫🇷

Paul

Great brunch here! Baked goods to die for. French onion soup perfectly balanced. Fresh orange juice and delicious coffees. Beef bourginon for breakfast is a great idea! Sat outside and talked to other diners. Had a great time. Seating inside is small tables close together but the food is so good it doesn't matter.
Delightful ambiance with comfortable shaded outdoor seating. And the pastries are top-notch! Also, good omelettes for breakfast.

Renaissance Patisserie is guaranteed to take you to the heart of France with their elegant aromas, sounds, and atmosphere. Their award-winning pastries are guaranteed to capture your heart as well.

Make sure you try their giant lychee macaron! It’s literally the size of my hand, and the depth of flavors was a pleasant surprise! The macaron was baked beautifully with a crisp outer layer and a soft, warm inner layer. The filling itself had incredible depth, and the lychee flavor was extremely profound. They did not skimp out on the lychee flavor at all, which was pleasantly surprising. To top it off (quite literally), they added a gold-dusted raspberry on top! At a price point of about $7.50, I think this pastry was definitely worth it.

They also serve a wide variety of sandwiches, soups, stews, and even provide family-portion takeaway meals, in addition to their extensive coffee selection.

FOLLOW @zackaryeats on Instagram and Tiktok to discover more places in NC!

They made my custom birthday cake and it was amazing! It was dairy and gluten free and delicious everyone went crazy over it! Still can’t believe how delicious it was and not overly sweet. Thanks for the best cake ever.

My wife and I have visited Charlotte a few times over the past several years and ever since discovering the Renaissance Patisserie it has become our 1st (and last) destination on our trip.  It is a perfect place for a lite meal after hours of driving.  Everything that we have sampled off of the menu has been great!  On our way home we like to stop and fill up on pastries and macarons to bring back to family.  It is always a special treat and we look forward to it every trip.
